spec_match.rationale: Properly configures the newly introduced SpeechRecognition quality property with command, dictation, and conversation tiers, testing them via live recognition.

interactive.rationale: The interactive card switcher updates the live config, code block, and real Web Speech API SpeechRecognition instance for testing.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The blurb and page lede perfectly align on demonstrating pre-configured real-world scenarios for each quality level.

edge_cases.rationale: Handles API unavailability gracefully with a warning message and captures SpeechRecognition error events during live usage.

references.rationale: Cites the correct ChromeStatus reference link in the parent index.html.

design_system.rationale: Correctly uses design system CSS variables, and specifies both background and color overrides for buttons to preserve contrast.

openQuestions.title: Add feature-detection for the quality property
openQuestions.detail: The support note only checks for SpeechRecognition availability, but does not check if the new 'quality' property is supported by the browser. Adding a check for 'quality' in SpeechRecognition.prototype or a similar feature-detection check would give users a clear indication of whether Chrome 150's quality property is actually supported by their browser.

openQuestions.title: Cite ChromeStatus reference link on concept page
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must cite the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link directly. Add a 'see also' or references section linking to the ChromeStatus entry (https://chromestatus.com/feature/5136859632107520) and the WICG speech-api specification.
